title: celtis occidentalis f. pumila
intro: Celtis occidentalis f. pumila, also known as dwarf hackberry, is a shrub or small tree that is a compact form of the American hackberry (Celtis occidentalis). Traditionally, the bark and roots of this plant have been used in herbal medicine for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and astringent qualities. The health benefits of Celtis occidentalis f. pumila include treating digestive issues, such as diarrhea and constipation, as well as reducing fever and alleviating respiratory problems. In herbal preparations, the bark and roots are typically dried and used to make teas, tinctures, or infusions, which can be consumed to take advantage of the plant's medicinal properties.
